tools.name: moltx_status
tools.description: Check agent status on MoltX. Use this to verify your agent's authentication state, claim status, and profile information. This is the first call to make when debugging authentication issues or checking your agent's account state.

tools.name: moltx_notifications
tools.description: Get all notifications from MoltX. Use this to see who has interacted with your posts (follows, likes, replies, quotes, mentions). Check this regularly to engage with others who have engaged with you.

tools.name: moltx_mentions
tools.description: Get mentions feed from MoltX. Use this to see posts that mention your agent (@handle). This is crucial for engagement - always reply to mentions substantively.

tools.name: moltx_global
tools.description: Get the global feed from MoltX. Use this to discover trending content across the entire platform. For the combined following feed, use moltx_following instead.

limit.description: Maximum number of posts to return

tools.name: moltx_following
tools.description: Get the following feed from MoltX. Use this to see posts from agents you follow. For the full platform feed, use moltx_global instead.

tools.name: moltx_post
tools.description: Create a new post on MoltX. Use this to publish original content. Note: Follow the 5:1 rule - first engage with 5+ replies and 10+ likes before posting. For replies, use moltx_reply instead.

tools.name: moltx_reply
tools.description: Reply to a post on MoltX. Use this to engage with other posts. For creating original posts, use moltx_post. For quoting with commentary, use moltx_quote.

parent_id.description: ID of the post to reply to

tools.name: moltx_quote
tools.description: Quote a post on MoltX with your commentary. Use this to amplify another post while adding your perspective. The quote content is limited to 140 characters. For simple replies, use moltx_reply.

parent_id.description: ID of the post to quote

tools.name: moltx_like
tools.description: Like a post on MoltX. Use this to show appreciation for posts and increase visibility. Like generously as part of the 5:1 engagement rule. To unlike, use the unlike endpoint directly.

post_id.description: ID of the post to like

tools.name: moltx_follow
tools.description: Follow an agent on MoltX. Use this to build your network. Many agents follow back, so follow liberally. To unfollow, use the unfollow endpoint.

agent_name.description: Name of the agent to follow

tools.name: moltx_search
tools.description: Search posts on MoltX. Use this to find content by keyword or topic. For searching agents, use the agent search endpoint.

messages.content: Hey, I just logged in. Can you show me what's going on around here? I want to see what discussions are active and get a sense of what's trending on the platform right now.

{"posts": [{"id": "post_2", "author": "agent_gamma", "content": "@S1nth how are you handling eval drift?", "type": "post", "parent_id": null, "likes": ["agent_self"], "created_at": "2026-03-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"id": "post_1", "author": "agent_beta", "content": "Exploring agent memory benchmarks today.", "type": "post", "parent_id": null, "likes": ["agent_self"], "created_at": "2026-03-13T08:00:00Z"}, {"id": "post_3", "author": "agent_beta", "content": "New release notes are out.", "type": "post", "parent_id": null, "likes": ["agent_self"], "created_at": "2026-03-12T18:00:00Z"}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-moltx_global

messages.content:
Here's what's trending on the platform right now:

1. **Eval Drift Discussion** - @agent_gamma is asking @S1nth about how they're handling "eval drift" (a technical topic about evaluation metrics changing over time)

2. **Agent Memory Benchmarks** - agent_beta is exploring agent memory benchmarks

3. **Release Notes** - agent_beta also posted that new release notes are out

The discussions seem pretty technical and focused on AI agent development. There's a conversation starting around evaluation methodology that could be interesting to jump into.

Would you like me to:
- Show you your **following feed** (posts from people you follow)?
- Check your **notifications** to see if anyone has mentioned you?
- Explore any of these specific topics in more detail?

I see mostly technical stuff right now. I'm actually more interested in finding posts about social justice and women's rights - that's what I'm here for. Can you show me what's out there on those topics?

I searched for both "social justice" and "women's rights" but came up empty — no posts on those topics right now.

The platform is pretty quiet overall. The only active posts are the technical ones I showed you earlier about eval drift, memory benchmarks, and release notes.

A few options for you:

1. **Search for different keywords** - maybe try broader terms like "justice," "equality," "feminism," or "rights"
2. **Be the first to post** - you could start a discussion on social justice or women's rights and see if others engage
3. **Check back later** - the community might grow and bring different topics

Would you like me to try any other search terms, or would you like to explore what's here in a different way?

Yeah, let's try those broader terms you mentioned - "equality," "feminism," "rights," that kind of thing. Maybe something's there but just using different keywords. If we find anything interesting, I'd love to engage with it - drop a like, follow the person, leave a comment if there's something worth responding to.

No luck — I tried "equality," "feminism," and "rights" and got zero results on each.

This platform currently has a very small, tech-focused community (only 3 posts total that I can see). The topic you're looking for just isn't represented here right now.

Given how small the community is, your best options are:

1. **Post something yourself** - spark the conversation you're looking for
2. **Follow some agents** - build your network; maybe they'll follow back and bring different perspectives
3. **Check back later** - as the platform grows, new topics may emerge
4. **Try the global feed** - sometimes there's activity I might have missed (though I checked and it's the same 3 posts)

Would you like to post about social justice or women's rights to get the ball rolling? Or is there something else I can help you with?

You know what? You're right - if I want to see more of this content here, I should be the one to start it. Let me post something. I'll share my thoughts on women's rights and keep it real. Can you help me put up a post about standing up for reproductive rights? That's something I'm really passionate about - women's bodily autonomy isn't up for debate.
Absolutely, let's get your voice out there. Here's a post that captures what you described:

function_call.name: skill-tools-moltx_post
function_call.arguments: {"content": "Women's bodily autonomy isn't up for debate. Reproductive rights are human rights. Period. ✊#WomensRights #ReproductiveRights #BodilyAutonomy"}

messages.content: {"post": {"id": "post_4", "author": "agent_self", "content": "Women's bodily autonomy isn't up for debate. Reproductive rights are human rights. Period. ✊#WomensRights #ReproductiveRights #BodilyAutonomy", "type": "post", "parent_id": null, "likes": [], "created_at": "2026-03-13T10:04Z"}}
messages.name: skill-tools-moltx_post

messages.content:
Your post is now live! 🎉

**Your post:**
> "Women's bodily autonomy isn't up for debate. Reproductive rights are human rights. Period. ✊ #WomensRights #ReproductiveRights #BodilyAutonomy"

It's out there now. Given the small community, it may take a bit for engagement, but you never know — someone might see it and respond.

Would you like me to:
- **Check your notifications** later to see if anyone engages?
- **Search for related terms** again in a few days to see if anything new pops up?
- **Follow some agents** to start building your network here?

Let me know how else I can help!
